operating_principle: Scanner v1 is a rules-governed diagnostic engine. It does not require live analytics integrations, does not claim exact causality, and does not claim guaranteed revenue improvement.
answer_scale_rule: If the answer is unknown because the signal is not measured, this affects Evidence Confidence. It must not be hidden inside a false score.

evidence_confidence_rules.separation_rule: Evidence Confidence is mandatory, is reported separately from ATI score, and never changes the score mathematically.

levels.name: Absent Evidence
levels.scanner_meaning: Required evidence is missing; no numeric score should be issued.
levels.assignment_rule: Assign E0 when the user cannot provide meaningful evidence for the vector.

levels.name: Self-Reported Evidence
levels.scanner_meaning: Mainly based on user perception or belief.
levels.assignment_rule: Assign E1 when answers are mainly based on judgment, memory, belief, or unverified observation.

levels.name: Observable Evidence
levels.scanner_meaning: Based on visible metrics, reports, or observed behavior.
levels.assignment_rule: Assign E2 when the user references visible metrics, analytics dashboards, reports, observable behavior, or consistent operational evidence.

levels.name: Multi-Source Evidence
levels.scanner_meaning: Based on multiple connected evidence sources.
levels.assignment_rule: Assign E3 when multiple evidence sources support the vector, such as analytics plus CRM, ad data plus site behavior, sales data plus lifecycle metrics, or cohort data plus retention data.

levels.name: Validated Evidence
levels.scanner_meaning: Based on repeated, tracked, verified data patterns.
levels.assignment_rule: Assign E4 only when evidence is tracked, repeated, validated, and stable over time. E4 does not permit guarantee language.

partial_profile_rule.statement: If one or more vectors are E0 and therefore unscorable, the scanner must not issue a clean full ATI score. It must issue a partial profile instead.
partial_profile_rule.allowed_output_template: ATI Profile: Partial / Scorable vectors: {list} / Unscorable vectors: {list} / E0 Absent Evidence
partial_profile_rule.prohibited_output_example: ATI Score: 61.5 (when any vector is E0)

input_categories.name: Funnel Context
input_categories.purpose: Understand the user's business model and funnel type

context_questions.question: What type of business is being assessed?
What is the main conversion goal?
Is the decision fast, medium, or long-cycle?
What are the main acquisition channels?
Is the model one-time, recurring, subscription, or enterprise?
What tools are used to measure funnel behavior?

evidence_questions.question: Do you have analytics data for traffic and engagement?
Do you track conversions or key actions?
Do you track leads, sales stages, or CRM progression?
Do you track repeat purchase, retention, churn, or usage?
Can you connect acquisition source to downstream value?
Is the data recent, repeated, and stable?

questions.question: Do your impressions, reach, or traffic produce qualified engagement?
Are the people you reach aligned with your actual offer or buyer profile?
Do users continue beyond first exposure into meaningful content or product exploration?
Does interest form quickly after first exposure?
Is the first landing experience clear enough to avoid confusion?
Do users avoid immediate bounce or early abandonment?
Does T1 traffic contribute to downstream commercial movement?
Can you identify which channel, audience, or message causes weak engagement?

failure_mode_triggers.trigger_pattern: High visibility, weak qualified engagement
Large reach with little commercial movement
Wrong traffic or poor buyer fit
Initial attention fades before interest forms
Users do not understand why the offer matters

questions.question: Does engagement show real interest rather than passive consumption?
Do users compare, save, revisit, inquire, or show preference-building behavior?
Does interest become intent within a reasonable time for your business model?
Is your value proposition clear enough to support desire?
Do interested users continue instead of fading away?
Does engaged traffic produce qualified leads, sales movement, or high-value actions?
Can you identify whether weak desire is caused by value, proof, comparison, or timing?
Do users move from learning to wanting?

failure_mode_triggers.trigger_pattern: Users consume content but do not form commercial intent
Users evaluate repeatedly without choosing
Offer does not become preferred against alternatives
Early intent fades before action
Users understand the category but not why this offer matters

questions.question: Are high-intent signals clearly visible before action?
Do users who show intent complete the next step?
Does action happen without excessive delay after intent forms?
Is the action path simple, credible, and low-friction?
Do users avoid abandoning forms, carts, demos, bookings, or checkout?
Does completed action create meaningful business value?
Can you identify whether action is blocked by trust, price, UX, complexity, or objections?
Are risk, proof, guarantee, or credibility concerns handled before action?

failure_mode_triggers.trigger_pattern: Action path is too hard, long, unclear, or broken
Desire exists but trust is insufficient
Price exposure collapses movement
Intent exists but urgency is weak
User cannot cross complexity threshold
Unresolved doubts block action

questions.question: Do post-action behaviors show continuity, not only completion?
Do customers return, repeat, continue, activate, or expand?
Does the relationship continue soon enough after first action?
Is onboarding or post-action experience clear and supportive?
Do customers avoid disappearing after first action?
Does loyalty generate repeat value, lifetime value, referral, or advocacy?
Can you identify what prevents repeat value or loyalty?
Do satisfied customers produce proof, referral, review, or public trust signals?

failure_mode_triggers.trigger_pattern: First action happens, continuity does not
Users disappear without visible alarm
First conversion is measured, loyalty is not
Loyalty does not become market-facing proof
Post-action relationship lacks structure

output_limits.rule: Scanner v1 must not output too many failure modes. Over-diagnosis weakens trust.

failure_mode_assignment.constraint_rule: Measurement Gap (tfo.constraint.measurement_gap) is assigned where evidence is insufficient. It affects Evidence Confidence and does not directly reduce ATI score.

output_model.full_score_condition: If all vectors are scorable, the scanner may issue a composite ATI score.
output_model.partial_profile_condition: If one or more vectors are E0, the scanner must issue a partial profile.
output_model.partial_output_template: ATI Profile: Partial / Scorable vectors: {list} / Unscorable vectors: {list} / E0 Absent Evidence
output_model.vector_output_template: {code} — {transition} / Score: {score} / {evidence_code} {evidence_name} / Class: {diagnostic_class} / Likely Failure Modes: {failure_modes} / Recommended Intervention: {intervention_layers}

allowed_examples: Your weakest vector is T2 — Interest → Desire. The likely failure mode is Value Ambiguity. This diagnosis is based on E1 Self-Reported Evidence and should be treated as provisional.
T3 appears Transition Constrained. The strongest signal points to Process Friction and Trust Deficit.
Measurement Gap limits the confidence of this diagnosis.
prohibited_examples: AI knows exactly why you are losing money.
Fixing this will increase revenue by 40%.
No data means your funnel is broken.

rules: show score and Evidence Confidence separately
do not make weak scores visually healthy
do not use gamified score effects
keep results readable without hidden interactions
